title: AIOps Incident Alert Correlation Dataset
subtitle: Synthetic SRE incident telemetry for AIOps alert correlation.
description: Synthetic incident and alert telemetry for practicing AIOps correlation, escalation prediction, and SRE dashboards.

resources.description: Root-level preview copy of AIOps Incident Alert Correlation Dataset.

fields.name: incident_id
fields.title: unique synthetic incident identifier

fields.name: event_timestamp
fields.title: UTC timestamp for the incident event

fields.name: service_name
fields.title: affected application or platform service

fields.name: gcp_region
fields.title: simulated Google Cloud region

fields.name: owning_team
fields.title: team responsible for the affected service

fields.name: severity
fields.title: incident severity level

fields.name: root_cause
fields.title: synthetic root cause category

fields.name: alert_count
fields.title: number of alerts emitted during the incident

fields.name: duplicate_alerts
fields.title: number of noisy duplicate alerts

fields.name: p95_latency_ms
fields.title: simulated p95 service latency in milliseconds

fields.name: error_rate_pct
fields.title: simulated request error rate percentage

fields.name: cpu_utilization_pct
fields.title: average CPU utilization during the incident

fields.name: memory_utilization_pct
fields.title: average memory utilization during the incident

fields.name: deployment_within_2h
fields.title: whether a deployment happened within two hours

fields.name: customer_impact_score
fields.title: synthetic impact score from 0 to 100

fields.name: mttr_minutes
fields.title: mean time to resolution in minutes

fields.name: escalated
fields.title: target label indicating whether the incident was escalated

resources.description: Column definitions, target label explanation, and suggested tasks.
Dataset quality summary with row count, schema, missing-value checks, and target distribution.

userSpecifiedSources: Synthetic dataset generated from a fixed-seed Python script included in this Kaggle package. No real users, cloud bills, incidents, prompts, credentials, or production systems are represented.
